title: Elaphandra sucrensis
intro: Elaphandra sucrensis, also known as Sucupira, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Elaphandra sucrensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sucupira include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
